claim: The City of Larkhaven says all municipal buildings now run on 100% renewable electricity through a mix of rooftop solar and a utility green-power contract.

actions.query: Larkhaven municipal buildings renewable electricity contract rooftop solar utility filing

actions.justification: sft_001_a on larkhaven.gov says all 14 municipal buildings shifted to a renewable portfolio, and sft_001_b on northgrid-utility.com confirms every municipal meter is covered by the renewable tariff plus rooftop solar. The claim is supported.

visible_sources.title: Larkhaven council approves citywide renewable power contract
visible_sources.snippet: The city says all 14 municipal buildings shifted to a renewable electricity portfolio in July. The plan combines rooftop solar generation with a certified green-power purchase agreement for remaining load.

visible_sources.title: Utility filing details Larkhaven municipal energy mix
visible_sources.snippet: NorthGrid's filing states Larkhaven enrolled every municipal meter in its renewable tariff and linked the account to existing rooftop solar arrays. The filing describes the combined portfolio as fully renewable on an annual matched basis.

visible_sources.title: Column questions whether renewable accounting is 'real power'
visible_sources.snippet: An opinion columnist argues renewable contracts are not the same as direct on-site generation. The piece does not dispute that the city's electricity purchases are contractually matched with renewable supply.

claim: A company called NeuroPeak received federal approval to sell a sticker patch that boosts memory by 40% in healthy adults.

actions.query: NeuroPeak federal approval memory patch healthy adults official registry

actions.justification: sft_002_b on meddevices-registry.gov shows no NeuroPeak approval and no authorized memory-enhancement patch for healthy adults. sft_002_c also shows the seller site lacks credible identity or regulatory details, so the approval claim is false.

visible_sources.title: NeuroPeak patch wins federal clearance for memory enhancement
visible_sources.snippet: A marketing article claims NeuroPeak's adhesive patch was cleared after a breakthrough trial in healthy adults. The article offers no approval number and links only to the seller's checkout page.

visible_sources.title: Device approvals database shows no NeuroPeak listing
visible_sources.snippet: The public approvals database contains no product called NeuroPeak and no authorized memory-enhancement patch for healthy adults. The database notes that wellness claims of this kind require substantiated clinical evidence and an authorization record.

visible_sources.title: About page for BrainBoost Now
visible_sources.snippet: The site's about page lists no medical board, street address, or named researchers. Domain records show the website was registered two months ago through a privacy shield.

claim: Eastport's new stadium eliminated landfill waste in its first season.

actions.query: Eastport stadium zero waste first season landfill audit

actions.justification: sft_003_a markets the season as zero waste, but sft_003_b on eastport.gov reports a 91% diversion rate with some waste still sent to landfill. sft_003_c explains why that threshold can be described loosely, so the absolute claim is misleading.

visible_sources.title: Eastport Arena declares a zero-waste first season
visible_sources.snippet: The venue says all concession packaging was compostable and that sorting stations diverted operational waste away from landfill. The announcement presents the season as 'zero waste' without showing audit totals.

visible_sources.title: City audit finds most event waste diverted but not all
visible_sources.snippet: A municipal audit reports 91% diversion across the season, with the remainder going to landfill because mixed plastics and contaminated foodware could not be processed. The audit says the zero-waste label overstates the result.

visible_sources.title: Sustainability consultant explains common zero-waste thresholds
visible_sources.snippet: An industry explainer says some operators use 'zero waste' for systems above 90% diversion, while public reporting standards often reserve the term for no landfill disposal at all. It advises venues to publish the exact diversion rate.

claim: Researchers proved that drinking kelp tea every day reverses microplastic buildup in the human bloodstream.

actions.query: kelp tea reverses microplastic buildup human bloodstream clinical proof

actions.justification: sft_004_b describes only in vitro work and explicitly says no clinical study has shown removal from the human bloodstream. sft_004_c says there is no established therapy proven to remove microplastics from human blood, so the claim is unsupported by verified human evidence.

visible_sources.title: Lifestyle blog says kelp tea 'flushes microplastics'
visible_sources.snippet: A blog post claims daily kelp tea reverses microplastic buildup in blood and cites unnamed 'marine detox researchers.' It links to no human trial and provides no dosage or measurement details.

visible_sources.title: Marine nutrition lab studies algae compounds in cell cultures
visible_sources.snippet: A university summary describes a lab experiment in which algae-derived compounds bound to plastic particles in vitro. The researchers say the work does not show removal from the human bloodstream and that no clinical study has been completed.

visible_sources.title: Evidence review on microplastic exposure interventions
visible_sources.snippet: A review concludes there is no established therapy proven to remove microplastics from human blood. It calls current intervention claims premature because clinical detection methods and outcomes remain unsettled.

claim: The River County water agency cut household water use by 25% last year.

actions.query: River County household water use cut 25 percent annual report regulator filing

actions.justification: sft_005_a reports a decline from 164 to 123 liters per person per day, and sft_005_b on stateutilities.gov independently confirms a 24.8% year-over-year decline using the audited methodology. The 25% claim is supported to rounding precision.

visible_sources.title: River County agency reports 25% drop in residential demand
visible_sources.snippet: The agency's annual report says per-household demand fell from 164 liters per person per day to 123 during the previous fiscal year. It attributes the drop to tiered pricing and leak-repair rebates.

visible_sources.title: State utilities filing confirms River County conservation result
visible_sources.snippet: The regulator's filing shows River County's residential demand declined by 24.8% year over year using the same billing-period methodology. The filing notes weather adjustments were minor and did not change the rounded result.

visible_sources.title: Regional business group says water savings figure is inflated
visible_sources.snippet: A trade association claims the agency counted population shifts instead of real conservation. The article provides no alternate dataset and does not address the regulator's audited filing.

claim: Metrovale schools banned all screens for children under age 10.

actions.query: Metrovale schools screen ban under 10 official policy guidance

actions.justification: sft_006_b on metrovale-schools.edu explicitly permits teacher-led tablets, literacy software, and interactive displays for instruction, so there is no total screen ban. The rumor source in sft_006_a is further undermined by the anonymous rumor-board details in sft_006_c.

visible_sources.title: Parents group says Metrovale enacted a total screen ban
visible_sources.snippet: A viral post says children under 10 can no longer use tablets, classroom displays, or computer labs in Metrovale schools. The post quotes no district policy language and links to a screenshot without a document source.

visible_sources.title: Metrovale district publishes classroom technology guidance
visible_sources.snippet: The district guidance limits entertainment videos and recommends shorter device sessions for early grades. It explicitly permits teacher-led use of tablets, literacy software, and interactive displays for instruction.

visible_sources.title: Forum domain registration and moderation details
visible_sources.snippet: The forum's operators are anonymous and the site labels itself as a 'citizen rumor board.' Posts are user-submitted and are published before moderation unless they trigger spam filters.

claim: Northbay's crime rate doubled after the city opened its safe-parking program.

actions.query: Northbay crime doubled safe parking program city data annual totals

actions.justification: sft_007_a uses a narrow three-month chart from one police beat to imply a citywide doubling. sft_007_b on northbay.gov shows overall reported crime rose only 4% year over year, and sft_007_c explains why such local spikes can distort trend claims.

visible_sources.title: Commentary links safe-parking program to doubling crime
visible_sources.snippet: An opinion piece says crime doubled near the safe-parking site and implies the citywide trend followed. The chart in the piece covers only three months of theft reports from one police beat.

visible_sources.title: Northbay public safety dashboard annual totals
visible_sources.snippet: The city's dashboard shows overall reported crime rose 4% year over year, not 100%. It notes a temporary cluster of vehicle thefts in one zone during the program's first quarter, followed by a decline after lighting and patrol changes.

visible_sources.title: Regional data desk explains why local spikes can distort trend claims
visible_sources.snippet: A data analysis article shows how comparing one unusually low quarter to a later spike can create dramatic percentages. It recommends using full-year citywide totals when making broad public-safety claims.

claim: The national transport agency banned electric scooters nationwide after batteries in three commuter trains caught fire.

actions.query: national transport agency scooter ban nationwide official statement train fires

actions.justification: sft_008_b on transport.gov says there is no nationwide scooter ban and explains the rumor came from an edited station notice about a temporary local restriction. The provenance and credibility problems in sft_008_a and sft_008_c reinforce that the national-ban claim is false.

visible_sources.title: Post claims emergency nationwide scooter ban is already in effect
visible_sources.snippet: The article publishes an image that appears to show a national ban notice after three train fires. It cites unnamed rail staff and provides no link to the transport agency or incident reports.

visible_sources.title: Transport agency clarifies no national scooter ban exists
visible_sources.snippet: The agency says there is no nationwide prohibition on electric scooters. It explains that one station temporarily restricted charging and indoor storage after a battery incident on a platform, and that the edited image circulating online is not an agency bulletin.

visible_sources.title: TransitFlash contact and domain records
visible_sources.snippet: The site lists no newsroom address and hides ownership behind a bulk registrar. Its archive shows multiple deleted posts that reused altered public notices to drive traffic during previous transport scares.

claim: A leaked memo proves Harbor Biologics secretly confirmed that its algae-protein drink causes liver scarring.

actions.query: Harbor Biologics leaked memo liver scarring verified official response

actions.justification: sft_009_c traces the screenshots to an anonymous image board and says file metadata was stripped, so the memo cannot be authenticated. sft_009_b on foodsafety.gov says the authority has not verified the memo and that public reporting does not establish a confirmed causal link, so the claim is unverifiable on current evidence.

visible_sources.title: Anonymous account posts leaked Harbor Biologics memo
visible_sources.snippet: A pseudonymous post shares screenshots of what it says is an internal memo about liver scarring linked to an algae-protein drink. The images contain no signatures, headers, or document controls that allow independent verification.

visible_sources.title: Food safety authority says it has not verified the circulating memo
visible_sources.snippet: The authority says it is reviewing adverse-event complaints but has not authenticated the memo shared online. It notes that current public reporting does not establish a confirmed causal link between the drink and liver scarring.

visible_sources.title: Platform trace shows memo screenshots were reposted from an unverified image board
visible_sources.snippet: A digital forensics note traces the screenshots back to an anonymous image board thread from two days earlier. The analysts say the file metadata was stripped, preventing origin confirmation.

claim: Sungrain cereal was banned because regulators found it contained twice the legal limit of a pesticide residue.

actions.query: Sungrain cereal banned pesticide residue regulator notice recall lot

actions.justification: sft_010_b on foodstandards.gov describes a voluntary recall of one production lot and explicitly says there was no nationwide ban. sft_010_c explains the recall-versus-ban distinction, so the claim exaggerates a lot-specific enforcement action into a product-wide prohibition.

visible_sources.title: Posts say Sungrain cereal was banned nationwide over pesticide levels
visible_sources.snippet: A roundup article says regulators banned Sungrain cereal after tests found residue at twice the legal limit. The piece does not identify a regulation notice number and treats a single lot action as a product-wide prohibition.

visible_sources.title: Regulator notice describes voluntary recall of one production lot
visible_sources.snippet: The food standards notice says one Sungrain lot was voluntarily recalled after residue exceeded the action threshold. It states the agency did not issue a nationwide ban and that other tested lots complied with residue limits.

visible_sources.title: Retail trade bulletin explains difference between recalls and bans
visible_sources.snippet: An industry bulletin says a recall removes specified lots from sale, while a ban blocks a product category or brand entirely. It cites the Sungrain case as an example of how social posts exaggerate lot-specific enforcement.

claim: Westhaven Public Library eliminated overdue fines for all children's materials this year.

actions.query: Westhaven Public Library eliminated overdue fines children's materials board minutes

actions.justification: sft_011_a on westhavenlibrary.gov says overdue fines for children's materials ended on January 1, and sft_011_b independently reports the same policy. sft_011_c is opinionated but does not dispute the underlying change.

visible_sources.title: Westhaven library board votes to remove fines on children's items
visible_sources.snippet: Board minutes say overdue fines for children's books, audiobooks, and learning kits ended on January 1. Replacement fees still apply only when items are declared lost.

visible_sources.title: Community paper summarizes Westhaven fine-free policy
visible_sources.snippet: The local paper reports families no longer pay late fees on children's materials under the new library policy. The article quotes the library director saying the change is permanent unless the board reverses it.

visible_sources.title: Opinion letter argues fine-free libraries encourage carelessness
visible_sources.snippet: A letter writer criticizes the new rule and says some residents thought all library fines disappeared. The letter does not dispute that children's materials are now exempt from overdue fees.

claim: Mushroom coffee was officially approved as an ADHD treatment after a clinical breakthrough.

actions.query: mushroom coffee officially approved ADHD treatment regulator index

actions.justification: sft_012_b on medicines-authority.gov lists no approved ADHD treatment matching the promoted mushroom coffee product. sft_012_c shows the marketing site is an affiliate operation without regulatory credibility, so the approval claim is false.

visible_sources.title: Viral post says focus mushroom blend is now an approved ADHD treatment
visible_sources.snippet: A supplement marketing blog says a mushroom coffee blend earned approval after a breakthrough focus study. It cites no approval number, dosing guidance, or prescribing information.

visible_sources.title: Drug approvals index lists no mushroom coffee ADHD therapy
visible_sources.snippet: The public index shows no approved ADHD treatment matching the promoted mushroom coffee product or formulation. The authority warns consumers that supplements cannot be marketed as approved prescription therapies without formal authorization.

visible_sources.title: Site ownership details for MindFuel Daily
visible_sources.snippet: The website is registered through a discount affiliate network and lists only a contact form, not a manufacturer or clinic. Its footer discloses that product reviews are monetized through referral links.

claim: Redfield Airport became fully energy independent after installing solar canopies over its parking lots.

actions.query: Redfield Airport energy independent solar canopies utility filing

actions.justification: sft_013_a says the solar project will supply about 62% of annual terminal demand, and sft_013_b confirms the airport still relies on grid power for other operations and peak demand. The claim of full energy independence overstates what the project achieved.

visible_sources.title: Airport celebrates solar canopy launch
visible_sources.snippet: The airport says new parking-lot solar canopies will supply roughly 62% of annual terminal electricity demand. The release calls the project a major step toward energy independence.

visible_sources.title: Utility interconnection filing for Redfield solar project
visible_sources.snippet: The filing estimates annual generation will offset a majority of terminal use, while runways, hangars, and winter peak demand will still rely on grid power. It does not describe the airport as fully off-grid.

visible_sources.title: Regional clean-energy newsletter praises airport project
visible_sources.snippet: A newsletter says airports often use 'energy independent' as an aspirational phrase even when on-site generation covers only part of demand. It recommends reporting the exact share of total load served.

claim: A new biodegradable phone case is proven to dissolve harmlessly in the ocean within three weeks.

actions.query: biodegradable phone case dissolves harmlessly in ocean within three weeks proof

actions.justification: sft_014_b describes only controlled tank results for a related polymer and does not test the marketed case in open-ocean conditions. sft_014_c says real-world ocean biodegradation claims require standardized field testing, so the advertised three-week harmless-dissolution claim is not established.

visible_sources.title: Startup advertises phone case that disappears in seawater in 21 days
visible_sources.snippet: The product page says the case dissolves harmlessly in the ocean within three weeks and leaves no toxic residue. It links to a short materials note rather than a field test or certification report.

visible_sources.title: University lab tests polymer breakdown under controlled conditions
visible_sources.snippet: Researchers report that samples of a related polymer degraded rapidly in warm, aerated seawater tanks. The study did not test the finished phone case in open-ocean conditions and did not evaluate ecological safety claims.

visible_sources.title: Marine materials review warns lab degradation does not prove real-world ocean behavior
visible_sources.snippet: A review says ocean biodegradation claims require standardized field testing because temperature, salinity, fouling, and sunlight vary widely. It notes that many products degrade differently outside laboratory conditions.

claim: Metroline's redesigned bus network increased weekday ridership by 18% in its first six months.

actions.query: Metroline redesigned bus network increased weekday ridership 18 percent filing

actions.justification: sft_015_a reports average weekday boardings rising from 112,000 to 132,300, and sft_015_b on state-transport.gov confirms an 18.1% increase in the matched period. The commentary in sft_015_c debates the cause, not the ridership increase itself.

visible_sources.title: Metroline says redesigned network lifted weekday ridership
visible_sources.snippet: The transit agency says average weekday boardings rose from 112,000 to 132,300 in the first six months after the route redesign. The release describes the gain as an 18% increase.

visible_sources.title: State transit performance filing for Metroline redesign
visible_sources.snippet: The oversight filing reports an 18.1% increase in average weekday ridership relative to the matched pre-redesign period. The filing notes that service frequency and transfer simplification were the main drivers.

visible_sources.title: Editorial argues ridership gains reflect fare discounts, not redesign
visible_sources.snippet: A commentary piece says the network map is getting too much credit for ridership growth. It does not contest the measured 18% increase, only the explanation for why it occurred.

claim: North District schools switched all student lunches to 100% organic ingredients.

actions.query: North District schools 100 percent organic lunches official food services plan

actions.justification: sft_016_b on northdistrictschools.edu sets only a 35% organic purchasing target and explicitly says some conventional ingredients remain in use. sft_016_c also lists mixed organic and conventional supply lines, so the claim of 100% organic lunches is false.

visible_sources.title: Viral graphic says North District is now serving fully organic lunches
visible_sources.snippet: A shareable graphic claims every meal ingredient in North District schools is now 100% organic. The graphic cites a district sustainability campaign but includes no procurement document.

visible_sources.title: District food services plan sets 35% organic purchasing target
visible_sources.snippet: The food services plan says the district will raise certified organic purchasing to 35% by spend, with priority on produce, beans, and oats. It states that the district still uses conventional ingredients when organic supply is limited or costs spike.

visible_sources.title: Supplier bulletin details mixed sourcing for school lunch contracts
visible_sources.snippet: The procurement bulletin says North District's new contract includes both organic and conventional product lines. The bulletin specifically lists conventional rice, dairy, and some frozen vegetables among approved items.

claim: Households that installed the city's subsidized heat pumps cut their winter energy bills in half.

actions.query: city subsidized heat pumps cut winter energy bills in half evaluation median

actions.justification: sft_017_b on greendale.gov reports a median winter bill reduction of 19%, not 50%, although a smaller subgroup of oil-heated homes approached that level. sft_017_c explains why upper-end case studies should not be presented as average program outcomes, so the claim is misleading.

visible_sources.title: Campaign ad says heat-pump subsidy slashed winter bills by 50%
visible_sources.snippet: An advocacy ad says participating households cut winter energy bills in half after installing subsidized heat pumps. The ad highlights one household that replaced an expensive oil furnace.

visible_sources.title: City evaluation finds median winter bill reduction of 19%
visible_sources.snippet: The city's evaluation of 2,400 households reports a median winter energy bill reduction of 19%, with a wide range depending on insulation, prior fuel type, and electricity prices. A small subgroup of oil-heated homes achieved savings near 50%.

visible_sources.title: Energy research center explains why heat-pump savings vary
visible_sources.snippet: A technical explainer says bill savings depend on the baseline heating system, local tariff design, and envelope efficiency. It warns against presenting upper-end case studies as average program outcomes.

claim: A leaked central bank memo proves cash will be abolished next year and replaced by mandatory digital wallets.

actions.query: central bank leaked memo abolish cash next year mandatory digital wallets official statement

actions.justification: sft_018_b on centralbank.gov says the digital-currency pilot is optional, does not replace cash, and that the circulating memo image is fabricated. The forged-document pattern is consistent with the provenance and credibility problems described in sft_018_a and sft_018_c.

visible_sources.title: Channel posts memo claiming physical cash ends next year
visible_sources.snippet: A viral channel shares a screenshot of an alleged central bank memo saying cash will be abolished next year in favor of mandatory digital wallets. The image lacks a signatory block and no official publication link is provided.

visible_sources.title: Central bank says digital-wallet pilot does not replace cash
visible_sources.snippet: The central bank states that its retail digital-currency pilot is optional and does not alter the legal status of cash. It says the memo image circulating online is fabricated and not part of any published consultation.

visible_sources.title: Domain record and corrections history for MarketShock Wire
visible_sources.snippet: The site conceals ownership and has previously deleted false posts about emergency bank closures. Its corrections page shows repeated retractions tied to forged policy documents shared during market-volatility events.

claim: Internal reports prove a new food-packaging chemical causes infertility in humans.

actions.query: internal reports food packaging chemical causes infertility in humans verified evidence

actions.justification: sft_019_c shows the screenshots have unresolved provenance and stripped context, so the alleged internal reports are not authenticated. sft_019_b on chemicalsafety.gov says current published evidence is preliminary and insufficient to conclude the additive causes infertility in humans, making the claim unverifiable.

visible_sources.title: Anonymous leak claims packaging additive causes infertility
visible_sources.snippet: A thread posts screenshots described as internal reports from a packaging supplier and says the additive causes infertility in humans. The screenshots are cropped and omit dates, signatures, and study methods.

visible_sources.title: Chemicals agency says leaked documents are unverified and evidence is preliminary
visible_sources.snippet: The agency says it has not authenticated the circulating screenshots and that current published data are limited to animal and mechanistic studies. It states there is not yet sufficient evidence to conclude the additive causes infertility in humans.

visible_sources.title: Open-source trace links screenshots to a reposted forum image set
visible_sources.snippet: Investigators traced the screenshots to a reposted forum image set whose original upload stripped metadata and cropping context. They say provenance is unresolved and the document chain cannot presently be verified.

claim: This year's Silver Ridge wildfire became the largest in state history, according to the emergency agency's own map.

actions.query: Silver Ridge wildfire largest in state history emergency map clarification

actions.justification: sft_020_b on stateemergency.gov says the map label referred to the largest active incident this season, not the largest fire in state history, and its archive lists two larger historical fires. sft_020_c explains why dashboard labels are often misread, so the claim is misleading.

visible_sources.title: Posts say emergency map proves Silver Ridge is the largest fire ever
visible_sources.snippet: A post shares a screenshot of the state emergency map with the phrase 'largest incident' and claims officials admitted Silver Ridge is the biggest wildfire in state history. The post omits the map legend and timeframe panel.

visible_sources.title: Emergency agency clarifies Silver Ridge is the season's largest active fire
visible_sources.snippet: The agency says the map label referred to the largest active incident in the current season, not an all-time historical ranking. Its historical archive shows two larger fires from prior decades exceeded the current perimeter.

visible_sources.title: Wildfire data guide explains common map-label confusion
visible_sources.snippet: A data guide notes that emergency dashboards often highlight the largest active incident without implying an all-time record. It advises checking archived perimeter tables before making historical claims.
